text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Gold City shares her thoughts with me, realizing
all her choices have been driven by the
expectations of those around her—whether trying
to meet them or to rebel against them.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
She says she wasn't making decisions on her own,
that it's because she's an empty doll that she has
no answer to the question of why she runs.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I reassure her that it won't be the end of the world
if she decides to stop running. But even then, she
can't bring herself to say she'll quit.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I urge her to run to her heart's content. With all
her strength, she dashes around the track, her
whole heart and mind thrust into each step.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Even if it's painful, even when it's tough, the
reason she runs is without a doubt because she
wants to.
